steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $\sqrt{\frac{6x - 5z}{30x^{2}z^{2}}}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Clear fractions by multiplying by the LCD
steps.content:
The equation is $\frac{1}{6xy}+xyz=\frac{1}{5yz}$. The least common denominator is $30xyz$, so multiply every term by $30xyz$: $$30xyz\left(\frac{1}{6xy}\right)+30xyz(xyz)=30xyz\left(\frac{1}{5yz}\right).$$ The three terms simplify as follows:
  • $30xyz\left(\frac{1}{6xy}\right)=5z$
  • $30xyz(xyz)=30x^2y^2z^2$
  • $30xyz\left(\frac{1}{5yz}\right)=6x$
So the equation becomes $$5z+30x^2y^2z^2=6x.$$

steps.title: Rearrange to isolate y squared
steps.content:
Isolate the $y^2$ term on the left:
$$30x^2y^2z^2 = 6x - 5z$$
Divide both sides by $30x^2z^2$:
$$y^2 = \frac{6x - 5z}{30x^2z^2}$$

steps.title: Take the square root to find y
steps.content:
Since $y$ is positive, take the positive square root of both sides:
$$y = \sqrt{\frac{6x - 5z}{30x^2z^2}}$$
This matches - B is correct.
